status_history.trigger: alt path 1 (int4) dispatch — 16GB downloaded clean, H1 mfs throughput cap REJECTED; H2 ingress quota promoted to priority 1; orthogonal cuInit=999 cuda issue surfaced (separate from gemma blocker)

Mk.XII Phase 3b pod 3csbgbnf2hf3gg (Llama-3.1-70B int4 unsloth) — ChunkedEncodingError IncompleteRead at 5.35GB / 6.94GB shard, ~40GB cumulative pod download. Different fail signature (HTTP connection broken visible at requests layer) but SAME ~40GB cumulative threshold. 3rd independent reproduction across 2 model families (gemma + Llama). H2 cumulative-bytes-per-pod-lifetime CONFIRMED. Implication: per-pod download ceiling ~35-40GB; single-shot 70B fp16 (140GB) impossible without persistent volume / multi-pod relay / HF-direct-mount. evidence: state/v11_mk_xii_phase3b_llama/verdict.json

axis: Mk.XII Phase 3a gemma family-internal scale closure (gemma-2-27b cell)
summary: gemma-2-27b CMT v3 measurement on RunPod H100 SXM 80GB pods has silently terminated 3 consecutive times during weight fetch, despite 3 independent root-cause-targeted fixes. Pattern points to runpod platform-level fault (mfs network filesystem hiccup OR region-specific quota OR oversize blob ingest quota), NOT memory/xet/disk.

three_strike_pattern.fix_applied: default loader, container disk 50GB, default memory budget
three_strike_pattern.outcome: silent SIGKILL during from_pretrained() ~360s, 59GB HF cache (~75% download)
three_strike_pattern.first_diagnosis: host RAM OOM (default device_map loaded full fp16 in CPU RAM)

three_strike_pattern.outcome: silent platform termination at xet 75% (18/24 files, 94GB cached) ~513s
three_strike_pattern.diagnosis_revision: OOM REJECTED — memcg.peak=104GB / 251GB cap (42%), oom_kill=0; 2015GB host RAM observed; reframed as platform-level (mfs OR safetensors fetch OR fetcher segfault on 22+GB shards)

three_strike_pattern.fix_applied: HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 + huggingface_hub.snapshot_download(max_workers=1, resume_download=True) + sys.excepthook + retry-with-backoff + per-shard sentinel + 251GB RAM
three_strike_pattern.outcome: (a) ModuleNotFoundError(transformers) caught by sys.excepthook → JSON written → agent relaunched after pip install; (b) relaunch 222s after fetching 12% (4/34 files) → pod ssh refused (auto-terminate by platform)
three_strike_pattern.diagnosis_revision: xet REJECTED (HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 active, sequential download confirmed via max_workers=1); platform-level fault confirmed across BOTH xet and non-xet paths

three_strike_pattern.fix_applied: pre-quantized base variant unsloth/gemma-2-27b-bnb-4bit (15.8GB vs 54GB fp16) + HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 + snapshot_download(max_workers=1)
three_strike_pattern.outcome: DOWNLOAD STAGE: SUCCESS — 15.82GB downloaded in 293.7s (54 MB/s avg sustained, NO platform termination, NO silent kill); LOAD STAGE: FAIL on cuInit=999 (RunPod cold-start CUDA driver mismatch) — orthogonal to gemma blocker; pod self-terminated by helper modprobe diagnostic attempt
three_strike_pattern.diagnosis_revision: H1 (mfs throughput cap) REJECTED — 16GB sustained download is inconsistent with throughput cap. H2 (RunPod ingress quota >=40-50GB threshold) PROMOTED to priority 1. Pattern: 27B fp16 (54GB) crosses quota; 4bit (16GB) does not.

ruled_out_root_causes: host RAM OOM (memcg evidence: oom_kill=0, peak=42% of 251GB)
xet (HuggingFace Rust accelerator) panic (v3 used HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 + sequential, same termination)
HF Hub API rate limit (single-thread sequential should not trigger; v3 still failed)
remaining_hypotheses: (H1) [REJECTED via alt-path-1-int4 download success] mfs (network filesystem mfs#us-ne-1.runpod.net:9421) sustained-write throughput cap triggers RunPod platform termination on extended download — REJECTED: int4 pre-quantized 16GB sustained 54 MB/s with NO termination on the same pod template

(H2) [PROMOTED priority 1] RunPod has undocumented network ingress quota per pod, hit ~40-50GB → SIGKILL by runpod orchestrator — supported by: int4 (16GB) PASS, fp16 v1 crash at 59GB / v2 crash at 94GB / v3 crash at relauch ~6GB after v2's same-region quota exhaustion
(H3) [PARTIAL test, ambiguous] RunPod region us-ne-1 specific issue; alt-path-1-int4 ran in EUR-NO-2 (DE) and download succeeded — but int4-vs-fp16 confound prevents disambiguation
(H4) Pod template (pytorch:2.4.0-py3.11-cuda12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04) has resource arbiter that incorrectly classifies long-running file write as 'idle' or 'abusive'
(H5) gemma-2-27b specifically (not other 27B+ models) triggers a HF Hub or RunPod-side filter — potentially license-related (gemma is google-licensed, gated previously)

alternative_paths.priority: TESTED (download phase) — partial pass, blocked by orthogonal cuInit=999 cuda issue at load stage; re-dispatch with cuda fix is informative but separate-axis raw#10 caveat
alternative_paths.name: int8_fallback
alternative_paths.approach: snapshot_download with pre-quantized base variant unsloth/gemma-2-27b-bnb-4bit; from_pretrained loads as 4bit directly; ~16GB download bypasses any throughput-based cap; tested 2026-04-27
alternative_paths.test_result: DOWNLOAD STAGE PASSED (15.82GB / 293.7s clean); LOAD STAGE FAILED (cuInit=999 — separate RunPod cold-start cuda issue, not gemma fault)
alternative_paths.h1_test_outcome: REJECTED H1 — throughput cap is not the platform termination cause
alternative_paths.raw_10_caveat: int4 quantization introduces measurement-regime shift vs fp16 baseline; raw#12 frozen-spec violation — measurement on SEPARATE axis from 2b/9b fp16; mitigation: re-measure 2b/9b in int4 too for parallel-axis comparison

alternative_paths.name: persistent_network_volume_cache
alternative_paths.approach: provision RunPod network volume (paid persistent storage), pre-download gemma-27b weights ONCE via dedicated download pod, then attach volume to measurement pod; survives pod recreation
alternative_paths.raw_10_caveat: additional cost (~$0.10-0.15/GB-month for ~60GB = ~$8/month); requires provisioning ahead

alternative_paths.name: different_region_or_template
alternative_paths.approach: try us-east, eu-central, or different pytorch image (e.g., pytorch:2.5.0+ or 2.8.0+) to test if H4/H3 hypotheses
alternative_paths.raw_10_caveat: still uses same RunPod platform; if H1-H2 are root cause, region change won't help

alternative_paths.name: offline_predownload_rsync
alternative_paths.approach: huggingface-cli download to local Mac (~54GB on local disk), then rsync over SSH to pod /workspace; bypasses RunPod's mfs ingest path entirely
alternative_paths.raw_10_caveat: very slow (Mac WAN upload-bound, 50-100 Mbps typical = ~100-180 min upload), but bypasses platform fault if H1-H2

alternative_paths.name: llama_family_pivot
alternative_paths.approach: task #6 HF Llama-3.1-8B/70B license unblock → use Llama family-internal as 2nd clean evidence path instead of gemma
alternative_paths.raw_10_caveat: depends on user manual action (task #6 pending); does not directly resolve gemma blocker

decision_criteria_for_resolution: 1+ alternative path produces successful gemma-2-27b measurement (cell PASS with rel_std/sat_frac/informative metrics)
OR Mk.XII Phase 3b 70B path is satisfied via Llama family-internal alternative (1/3 → 2/3 clean) without needing gemma-27b
OR pre-registered hypotheses HMK-A/HMK-B/HQS-C are formally dropped/revised via raw#12 re-pre-register cycle (with new gemma-2-27b-free protocol)

raw_45_omega_blocker_metadata.next_retry_requires: alt path 2 (persistent network volume) dispatched OR alt path 1 (int4) re-run with cuInit fix; this blocker remains active until gemma-2-27b cell measured (fp16 OR explicitly accepted int4 separate-axis cell)

raw_45_omega_blocker_metadata.h2_status: PROMOTED priority 1 — to be tested via alt path 2 (network volume)
